message:
test(storage): pin Hybrid ready sort + limit through priority bucket split

Regression test that exercises the new bucket-split Hybrid path
end to end, with concrete output assertions. Without this, a
future refactor of the bucket dispatcher could quietly reorder
results or drop limit semantics and only blow up under
production load.

Setup: four open issues with deliberate priority/age permutations:

  bd-low-old      P4  t=base+0
  bd-high-old     P1  t=base+1
  bd-critical-new P0  t=base+2
  bd-low-new      P2  t=base+3

Two assertions:

1. Unbounded `Hybrid` query returns:
     bd-high-old, bd-critical-new, bd-low-old, bd-low-new

   - High bucket (priority <= 1) ordered by created_at ASC:
     bd-high-old (P1, t=1) before bd-critical-new (P0, t=2).
     P0 is "more critical" than P1, but Hybrid intentionally
     groups all High together by age — proves that the split path
     does NOT re-rank inside the High bucket by priority.
   - Low bucket (priority > 1) ordered by created_at ASC:
     bd-low-old (P4, t=0) before bd-low-new (P2, t=3) — same
     story for the Low bucket: age, not priority.

2. With `ReadyFilters { limit: Some(2), .. }`, the result is the
   first two High-bucket items: bd-high-old, bd-critical-new. The
   bucket dispatcher must short-circuit the Low query entirely
   when the High query already filled the limit; this assertion
   pins that.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -13065,6 +13065,70 @@ mod tests {
         assert_eq!(ready[0].id, "bd-legacy-ready");
     }
 
+    #[test]
+    fn test_get_ready_issues_hybrid_sort_and_limit() {
+        let mut storage = SqliteStorage::open_memory().unwrap();
+        let base = Utc.with_ymd_and_hms(2026, 3, 12, 0, 0, 0).unwrap();
+
+        for issue in [
+            make_issue("bd-low-old", "Low old", Status::Open, 4, None, base, None),
+            make_issue(
+                "bd-high-old",
+                "High old",
+                Status::Open,
+                1,
+                None,
+                base + chrono::Duration::seconds(1),
+                None,
+            ),
+            make_issue(
+                "bd-critical-new",
+                "Critical new",
+                Status::Open,
+                0,
+                None,
+                base + chrono::Duration::seconds(2),
+                None,
+            ),
+            make_issue(
+                "bd-low-new",
+                "Low new",
+                Status::Open,
+                2,
+                None,
+                base + chrono::Duration::seconds(3),
+                None,
+            ),
+        ] {
+            storage.create_issue(&issue, "tester").unwrap();
+        }
+
+        let ids: Vec<String> = storage
+            .get_ready_issues(&ReadyFilters::default(), ReadySortPolicy::Hybrid)
+            .unwrap()
+            .into_iter()
+            .map(|issue| issue.id)
+            .collect();
+        assert_eq!(
+            ids,
+            ["bd-high-old", "bd-critical-new", "bd-low-old", "bd-low-new"]
+        );
+
+        let limited_ids: Vec<String> = storage
+            .get_ready_issues(
+                &ReadyFilters {
+                    limit: Some(2),
+                    ..ReadyFilters::default()
+                },
+                ReadySortPolicy::Hybrid,
+            )
+            .unwrap()
+            .into_iter()
+            .map(|issue| issue.id)
+            .collect();
+        assert_eq!(limited_ids, ["bd-high-old", "bd-critical-new"]);
+    }
+
     #[test]
     fn test_get_ready_issues_excludes_in_progress() {
         let mut storage = SqliteStorage::open_memory().unwrap();
